slices.title: Workflow state bootstrap
slices.goal: Introduce canonical slice tracking artifacts for roadmap-driven work.
slices.summary: Create machine-readable slice state and a human-readable roadmap before the first product slice.
acceptance: Canonical slice state exists in .codex/slices.json.
Human-readable roadmap exists in docs/ROADMAP.md.
Current and completed slices are explicit.
slices.notes: Bootstrapped as part of repository initialization.

slices.title: Python CLI scaffold baseline
slices.goal: Create the minimal installable Python CLI scaffold with config init and validation.
slices.summary: Add pyproject, src layout, typed config domain models, JSON persistence, and CLI commands.
acceptance: python -m fqdn_updater --help works.
fqdn-updater init writes a valid scaffold config.
fqdn-updater config validate accepts the scaffold and rejects invalid files.
Ruff and pytest can be run once dependencies are installed.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Config domain model expansion
slices.goal: Deepen typed config models and validation rules toward router and mapping UX.
slices.summary: Extend config schema with stricter contracts while staying local-only.
acceptance: Router, service, and mapping config rules are locally validated.
Validation errors are actionable and deterministic.
slices.notes: Completed with stronger slug, list uniqueness, source URL presence, and allowed_source_ips validation on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Source registry scaffold
slices.goal: Introduce explicit service source registry contracts without fetching data yet.
slices.summary: Add typed source definitions and initial registry wiring.
acceptance: Supported services are modeled explicitly.
Registry access stays out of CLI formatting code.

slices.title: Logging and run artifact scaffold
slices.goal: Prepare machine-readable run artifacts and logging boundaries.
slices.summary: Add typed run result skeletons and artifact persistence abstractions.
acceptance: Run artifact models exist.
Artifact writing lives in infrastructure.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: RCI client contracts
slices.goal: Define transport-facing interfaces and RCI-only client contracts.
slices.summary: Add infrastructure interfaces without implementing sync behavior yet.
acceptance: RCI client boundaries are explicit.
No HTTP details leak into domain or application.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Workflow enforcement gates
slices.goal: Add enforced verification and repo-local workflow rules for slice execution.
slices.summary: Introduce a canonical verify wrapper, CI gate, and explicit edit autonomy and sandbox rules.
acceptance: A single verify wrapper exists and runs the required checks.
CI executes the verify wrapper on push and pull request.
Repository instructions explicitly allow non-destructive in-workspace edits without extra confirmation.
Repository instructions explicitly state that sandbox is still expected.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Fetch And Normalize Pipeline
slices.goal: Fetch raw service sources and normalize them into deterministic domain entries.
slices.summary: Add the application service, domain normalization contracts, and HTTP raw source fetching for enabled service sources.
acceptance: Raw source text can be fetched behind an infrastructure adapter.
Source entries are normalized deterministically by format.
Enabled services are loaded into a stable source report.
Failures are isolated per service/source and reported cleanly.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Deterministic Managed Diff Planning
slices.goal: Plan managed object-group diffs deterministically before any apply path exists.
slices.summary: Add a pure object-group diff model and an application planner for one managed mapping without transport or apply logic.
acceptance: Managed object-group diffs are deterministic and typed.
Planning rejects unmanaged mappings.
Route-binding intent is captured without route-state diffing.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Read-Only Sync Orchestration
slices.goal: Assemble the first dry-run orchestration flow without any router writes.
slices.summary: Add read-only orchestration over enabled routers and managed mappings, resolve router secrets, read current object-group state, build deterministic service plans, aggregate statuses, and persist run artifacts.
acceptance: Dry-run orchestration processes enabled routers, enabled services, and managed mappings only.
Source loading happens once per run and service failures are isolated cleanly.
Router secret resolution and object-group reads are isolated per router and per mapping.
Run artifacts are persisted with deterministic ordering and status aggregation.
No router write methods are called anywhere in the new flow.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: RCI Read Transport Implementation
slices.goal: Implement the first real Keenetic RCI read path for object-group state and DNS proxy status.
slices.summary: Replace the current RCI client stub for read-only operations with an HTTP Digest-backed client that can execute RCI requests, parse managed object-group state, and expose DNS proxy status without any write behavior.
acceptance: KeeneticRciClient can issue authenticated HTTPS requests to the configured /rci endpoint.
get_object_group returns deterministic ObjectGroupState for existing and missing groups.
get_dns_proxy_status returns a typed DnsProxyStatus from router data.
HTTP, Digest, and payload details remain fully inside infrastructure.
No write methods are implemented in this slice.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: CLI Dry-Run Entry Point
slices.goal: Expose the read-only orchestration flow as a user-facing dry-run command with deterministic output and exit codes.
slices.summary: Add a CLI dry-run command that loads config, invokes the orchestration flow, renders human-readable and JSON output, writes artifacts, and returns stable exit codes for no-changes, planned changes, and errors.
acceptance: A dry-run CLI command exists and accepts a config path.
The command uses the real RCI read path and existing orchestration service.
Human-readable and JSON output modes are supported.
Exit codes distinguish no changes, planned changes, and failures.
No router write methods are called in any dry-run path.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Managed Object-Group Apply Core
slices.goal: Implement safe managed-only object-group mutation through the RCI client.
slices.summary: Add the first write path for object-group creation and entry add/remove operations, preserving read-before-write behavior and avoiding route changes for now.
acceptance: KeeneticRciClient can ensure a target object-group exists.
KeeneticRciClient can add and remove entries for a managed object-group.
Write commands are skipped when the diff is empty.
Write batching is deterministic and bounded for larger diffs.
No route binding writes are introduced in this slice.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-09.

slices.title: Apply Orchestration And Sync CLI
slices.goal: Add a user-facing sync command that applies managed object-group diffs across routers.
slices.summary: Build the apply orchestration over the existing planning flow, call the new object-group write methods, persist artifacts, and expose the result through a sync CLI command with predictable exit behavior.
acceptance: A sync CLI command exists and loads the configured routers and mappings.
Sync reuses read-before-write planning before any mutation.
Managed object-group changes are applied across multiple routers with failure isolation.
A successful apply run persists artifacts and returns deterministic exit codes.
Route binding writes are still out of scope in this slice.
slices.notes: Delivers the first end-to-end working sync for object-group contents only.

slices.title: Route Binding Read And Apply
slices.goal: Implement managed route binding support for configured object-groups.
slices.summary: Add typed route-state reads and route ensure behavior to the RCI layer, then extend apply orchestration to maintain configured route bindings with managed-only guarantees.
acceptance: Current route binding state can be read without leaking RCI payloads outside infrastructure.
Configured route bindings are created or updated only for managed mappings.
Object-group writes and route writes stay ordered and deterministic.
Save behavior is invoked only after successful apply work.
Failures in one mapping do not block remaining mappings and routers.
slices.notes: Closes a major PRD gap by making route management part of the production path.

slices.title: Run Logging And Rich Failure Artifacts
slices.goal: Add operator-grade logging and richer artifact detail for dry-run and sync runs.
slices.summary: Introduce text and JSON logging modes, capture run/session context, and enrich failure artifacts with step-level error detail needed for automation and manual diagnosis.
acceptance: Runs emit either text or JSON logs according to runtime config.
Run artifacts include enough failure detail to identify router, service, and step.
Dry-run and sync both use the same logging boundary and artifact conventions.
Logging stays outside core domain logic.
slices.notes: Makes the tool operationally legible instead of test-only.

slices.title: Status And Preconditions Diagnostics
slices.goal: Add user-facing status diagnostics for local config checks and remote Keenetic preconditions.
slices.summary: Expose a status command that reports local config readiness and remote router diagnostics such as DNS proxy availability and basic RCI access health, without mutating router state.
acceptance: A status CLI command exists.
The command reports config readiness and router connectivity or read failures per router.
DNS proxy status is surfaced in a typed, operator-readable way.
The command never mutates router state.
slices.notes: Covers the PRD need for practical diagnostics before and after sync runs.

slices.title: Config Management CLI
slices.goal: Provide first-class CLI workflows for router and mapping management without manual JSON editing.
slices.summary: Add focused config subcommands for listing and editing routers and mappings while preserving atomic config writes and existing schema validation.
acceptance: Router add and router list commands exist.
Mapping set or equivalent command exists for managed service routing configuration.
Config changes are written atomically through infrastructure persistence code.
User-facing validation errors remain actionable and deterministic.
slices.notes: Improves operator UX after the core sync path exists.

slices.title: Packaging And Scheduled Execution
slices.goal: Package the tool for repeatable VPS execution through Docker and systemd.
slices.summary: Add a minimal container entrypoint and example systemd service/timer assets that run the sync command as a one-shot scheduled job, plus concise operator documentation for deployment.
acceptance: A minimal Docker runtime path exists for the CLI tool.
Example systemd service and timer files are provided.
The scheduled execution path uses the sync CLI command as a one-shot job.
Deployment documentation explains config, secrets, artifacts, and logs.
slices.notes: Brings the repository up to the PRD’s intended VPS batch-job operation.
